response.justification: Wei Chen has genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrated low-latency distributed systems work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, but the core requirement — modern C++ or Rust proficiency — is effectively absent. Their only C++ exposure was a brief role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project; the last decade of their career is firmly in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. The routing tiling role demands deep systems-level C++ for performance-critical pipeline work, and there is no evidence this candidate can operate at that level.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development with GISCollective and Green Map, directly adjacent to the mapping/navigation domain
Low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and caching
Consistent Linux server application background across multiple roles and personal projects
concerns: No demonstrable modern C++ or Rust proficiency — last C++ work was 2014–2015 and limited in scope
No routing, navigation tile processing, or geospatial data pipeline experience at a production scale
Career trajectory for the past 10+ years is entirely web/TypeScript/Node.js, not systems or performance-critical C++ development
